description_long: This is a small, arched stone pedestrian bridge with a gently curved pathway and solid stone parapet railings on both sides. The walking surface is composed of large, rectangular stone slabs, some of which show subtle moss or weathering, giving the bridge an aged, historic appearance. The sides of the bridge feature a prominent arch structure made of tightly fitted stone blocks, supporting the span and creating an open space beneath. The parapet railings are solid and continuous, constructed from the same stone as the rest of the bridge, providing both safety and a cohesive visual style. The bridge is relatively narrow, suitable for foot traffic or small personal vehicles like bicycles. Its overall design is utilitarian yet classic, reminiscent of traditional European or park bridges.
description: A small, arched stone pedestrian bridge with solid stone parapet railings and a weathered, historic appearance. The bridge features a gently curved pathway and prominent stone arch supports.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone pathway and solid parapet railings, emphasizing the entry point and the width of the bridge.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the prominent stone arch structure beneath the bridge and the continuous stone parapet railings.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the stone pathway and parapet railings from the opposite end.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again displaying the stone arch and parapet railings, mirroring view 1.
attribute_railing_design: solid stone parapet with continuous rectangular profile and no decorative elements
